Query: "evolutionary divergence whole-genome vs small-scale duplications in yeast"
Now retrieving evidence...
After reviewing the retrieved evidence, I find that studies show whole-genome duplications in yeast lead to faster sequence divergence due to relaxed selection pressure and increased mutation accumulation compared to smaller scale duplications. Small-scale duplications tend to retain functional homology longer and undergo purifying selection more effectively.
Based on this evidence, I now agree with the initial conclusion that whole-genome duplications are indeed more divergent than small-scale duplications in yeast.
